title: Primes in arithmetic progressions to large moduli I: Fixed residue classes

thm:1.1.summary: Equidistribution of primes in fixed residue class $a \pmod{q_1 q_2}$ for double moduli with the polytope ($Q_1 Q_2^2 < x^{1-100\varepsilon}$, $Q_1^{12} Q_2^7 < x^{4-100\varepsilon}$, $Q_1^{20} Q_2^{19} < x^{10-100\varepsilon}$). Goes beyond Bombieri-Vinogradov for arbitrary (non-smooth) moduli.

cor:1.2.summary: For $0 < \delta < 1/42$, equidistribution holds for moduli $q \le x^{1/2+\delta}$ that admit a divisor $d$ in a specific intermediate range. Extracts a usable BV-beyond-$1/2$ statement from thm:1.1.

cor:1.3.summary: ALMOST-ALL moduli result: for $0 < \delta < 1/55$, all but at most $18\delta Q\,\varphi(a)/a$ moduli $q \in [Q, 2Q]$ (with $(q,a)=1$) satisfy $\pi(x;q,a) = (1+o(1))\,\pi(x)/\varphi(q)$. Bombieri-Friedlander-Iwaniec-style improvement to almost-all level $1/2 + 1/55$.

cor:1.4.summary: Compact double-modulus form: $Q_1 \le x^{1/21}$, $Q_2 \le x^{10/21-\varepsilon}$; equidistribution sums over $q_1 q_2$. The workhorse statement most directly comparable to Polymath8a (thm:C below).

thm:A.summary: Bombieri-Friedlander-Iwaniec (1986): for $\delta > 0$ and $Q = x^{1/2+\delta}$, the equidistribution sum over $q \in [Q, 2Q]$ is bounded by $\delta^2 x/\log x + $ lower-order. Cited as the BFI baseline.

thm:B.summary: Bombieri-Friedlander-Iwaniec (1986): well-factorable $\lambda_q$ at level $x^{4/7-\varepsilon}$ gives equidistribution. Cited as the BFI well-factorable baseline.

thm:C.summary: Zhang / Polymath: smooth-modulus equidistribution at level $x^{1/2+\delta}$ with $\delta < 7/300$. Cited verbatim — Polymath8a 1402.0811 thm:1.1.

def:1.summary: Siegel-Walfisz condition for a sequence $\alpha_n$: smooth equidistribution in residue classes with $\tau(d)^{B_0}$ losses.

prop:7.1.summary: Type II estimate at level satisfying eq:1.3, eq:1.4 with intermediate primes $P_j \ge x^{1/7+10\varepsilon}$. The combinatorial Type II input for thm:1.1.

prop:7.2.summary: Companion Type II estimate covering an alternative regime of the polytope.

prop:7.3.summary: Type III estimate handling the cubic-product regime (third constraint $Q_1^{20} Q_2^{19} < x^{10-100\varepsilon}$).

prop:7.4.summary: Final assembly proposition combining the Type II and Type III estimates into the equidistribution sum of thm:1.1.

edges.note: Sharpens BFI's $1/2 + \delta$ level by adding the divisor structure.
Strengthens BFI well-factorable from $4/7$ to a comparable explicit double-modulus regime without the well-factorable hypothesis.
Eliminates the smoothness / dense-divisibility hypothesis from Polymath8a's thm:1.1 (= thm:C here), at comparable level.

main_results.description: thm:1.1 (the polytope equidistribution) and four corollaries cor:1.2-1.4 extracting BV-beyond-$1/2$ consequences in successively cleaner forms.

context_baselines.description: thm:A (BFI 1986), thm:B (BFI well-factorable), thm:C (Polymath8a smooth moduli). The three predecessors being improved on or generalized.

type_estimates_engine.description: prop:7.1 (Type II), prop:7.2 (companion Type II), prop:7.3 (Type III), prop:7.4 (assembly). The combinatorial decomposition powering thm:1.1.

definitional_layer.description: def:1 — Siegel-Walfisz hypothesis for sequences, used pervasively.

Polymath8a.title: New equidistribution estimates of Zhang type
Polymath8a.role: predecessor — thm:C cites this; the Deligne-dependent smooth-modulus level being generalized

BombieriFriedlanderIwaniec.title: Primes in arithmetic progressions to large moduli I, II, III
BombieriFriedlanderIwaniec.role: predecessor — thm:A and thm:B cite the BFI series this paper is named after

Deligne:WeilII.title: La conjecture de Weil II
Deligne:WeilII.role: foundational — used in Type III estimate via Kloosterman bounds
